title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Dave McGillivray to run his 54th Boston Marathon in 2026 with three of his children and astronaut Sunita Williams.
McGillivray's family is deeply involved in the Boston Marathon, with each member contributing through running or working the event.
The 2026 race will honor Susan Hurley's memory and feature charitable fundraising by McGillivray's children for various causes.

title: Jayson Tatum, Derrick White sound off on ‘ridiculous’ Jaylen Brown ejection
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected before halftime in the Celtics' loss to San Antonio, sparking comments from teammates.
Jayson Tatum and Derrick White criticized the referee's decision, particularly the second technical foul on Brown.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla supported Brown's reaction, while Brown has not commented publicly since the incident.

title: He’s running Boston to honor of his mother’s adventurous spirit
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: James Hebert, 62, from Encinitas, Calif., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon honoring his late mother, Janice.
Hebert's mother inspired him with her adventurous spirit, having pursued activities like hang-gliding and skydiving.
Hebert will wear a lilac-colored shirt for the race, symbolizing his mother's love for lilacs and adventure.

title: Authorities look into possible vandalism of expensive geotubes on Nantucket
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Authorities investigate possible vandalism of geotubes on Nantucket after finding deep cuts, suspected to be deliberate.
Sconset Beach Preservation Fund offers $10,000 reward for information leading to arrest related to geotube damage.
Winter storms and alleged vandalism have severely compromised the geotube erosion-control system on Sconset Bluff, Nantucket.

title: ICE detains Marlborough 14-year-old, apparently as ‘hostage’ to target her father, Rep. Trahan says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rep. Lori Trahan alleges ICE used a 14-year-old Marlborough girl as 'hostage' to force her father's surrender.
The girl, with no criminal history, was detained in an overnight operation, prompting Trahan to demand her release.
Similar incident involved a 5-year-old in Minnesota, sparking backlash and criticism of ICE's enforcement tactics.

title: Mass. woman among victims in Utah triple homicide; fundraiser launched for husband
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Easthampton resident Natalie Graves, 34, was killed while hiking in Utah as part of a triple homicide incident.
The suspect, arrested after the killings, had fled an earlier crime scene and stole a vehicle to reach the trailhead.
A GoFundMe for Graves's husband, Taylor, nearing its goal, supports expenses from her death and estate settlement.

title: Here’s what to know about the new $1 billion Gillette headquarters planned for Fort Point
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: P&G is investing $1 billion to build a new Gillette headquarters at 232 A St., South Boston.
The new site will include the Grooming Headquarters and Technical Innovation Center, approved for a research and development facility.
Blade and razor production will move to a 200,000-square-foot facility in Andover, starting this year.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Dre'Mont Jones joins the New England Patriots, replacing K'Lavon Chaisson who signed with the Commanders.
Jones, with Ohio State and Titans ties, did not overlap with Patriots coach Mike Vrabel at any of his previous teams.
At 6-foot-3, 281 pounds, Jones offers versatility, capable of playing both as a defensive end and inside on the line.

title: Seamark at Encore Boston to close, less than a month after workers unionize
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Seamark Seafood & Cocktails will permanently close on March 29, citing economic challenges, affecting 56 workers.
UNITE HERE Local 26 claims the closure is retaliatory, following the restaurant workers' recent vote to unionize.
Seamark is accused of union-busting tactics; workers had voted 38-7 to join the union before the closure decision.

title: A nearly 100-year-old Catholic school in Medford is closing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: St. Joseph School in Medford, serving pre-K to eighth grade, will close after this school year due to declining enrollment.
Enrollment dropped to 169 students from 222 since 2021, with rising costs making operations unsustainable by the Archdiocese of Boston.
St. Joseph School's closure is part of a trend affecting religious schools in Greater Boston, including recent shutdowns of other Catholic schools.
